system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf   10;
        m       0.4;
        n       3;
        tauStar 1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              1e-03;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content="FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Bottom row (z = -0.07)
    (-1.0, -0.4, -0.07)   // 0
    (-0.6, -0.4, -0.07)   // 1
    (0.6, -0.4, -0.07)    // 2
    (1.0, -0.4, -0.07)    // 3
    // Top row (z = -0.07)
    (-1.0, 0.4, -0.07)    // 4
    (-0.6, 0.4, -0.07)    // 5
    (0.6, 0.4, -0.07)     // 6
    (1.0, 0.4, -0.07)     // 7
    // Bottom row (z = +0.07)
    (-1.0, -0.4, 0.07)    // 8
    (-0.6, -0.4, 0.07)    // 9
    (0.6, -0.4, 0.07)     // 10
    (1.0, -0.4, 0.07)     // 11
    // Top row (z = +0.07)
    (-1.0, 0.4, 0.07)     // 12
    (-0.6, 0.4, 0.07)     // 13
    (0.6, 0.4, 0.07)      // 14
    (1.0, 0.4, 0.07)      // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Left block: vertices 0,1,5,4 on bottom and 8,9,13,12 on top
    // Face 0,1,5,4: looking from left (negative x), y increases upward
    //   4(-1,0.4) --- 5(-0.6,0.4)
    //   0(-1,-0.4) --- 1(-0.6,-0.4)
    // Clockwise when viewed from left means normal points LEFT (outward)
    // So we reverse: 4->5->1->0 gives counterclockwise, normal pointing RIGHT (inward)
    // We want normal pointing LEFT (outward), so keep 0->1->5->4
    hex (0 1 5 4 8 9 13 12) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Middle-left block: vertices 1,2,6,5 on bottom and 9,10,14,13 on top
    // Face 1,2,6,5: looking from x between -0.6 and 0.6, y increases upward
    //   5(-0.6,0.4) --- 6(0.6,0.4)
    //   1(-0.6,-0.4) --- 2(0.6,-0.4)
    // Clockwise when viewed from right means normal points RIGHT (outward)
    // So keep 1->2->6->5
    hex (1 2 6 5 9 10 14 13) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Middle-right block: vertices 5,6,10,9 on bottom and 13,14,15,12 on top?
    // Wait, let me reconsider the block layout. Looking at offsetCylinder,
    // we need blocks that cover the cylinder cross-section. Let me redefine:
    // Block 2: vertices 4,5,9,8 on bottom and 12,13,13?,12?
    // Actually, let me use the standard approach with two blocks across x:
    // But the geometry has 4 blocks. Let me trace through each carefully.

    // Block 2: vertices 4,5,9,8 on bottom and 12,13,13?,12?
    // Bottom face 4->5->9->8: 4(-1,0.4), 5(-0.6,0.4), 9(-0.6,-0.4), 8(-1,-0.4)
    // Looking from left, y-up: clockwise -> normal points left (outward) ✓
    // But 4->5->9->8 is clockwise when viewed from left, so normal points left (outward) which is correct
    // However, OpenFOAM wants the face vertices in right-hand-rule order with normal pointing OUTWARD
    // For face 4,5,9,8: 4(-1,0.4), 5(-0.6,0.4), 9(-0.6,-0.4), 8(-1,-0.4)
    // Vector 4->5 = (0.4, 0), 5->9 = (0, -0.8)
    // Normal = (0.4, 0, 0) × (0, -0.8, 0) = (0, 0, -0.32) pointing in -z
    // But we're in 2D, checking x-y plane... Let me just trust the OpenFOAM check.
    // The safest approach is to use the convention from the original working cases:
    hex (4 5 9 8 12 13 14 15) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: vertices 5,6,10,9 on bottom and 13,14,15,12 on top
    // Bottom face 5->6->10->9: 5(-0.6,0.4), 6(0.6,0.4), 10(0.6,-0.4), 9(-0.6,-0.4)
    // Looking from right, y-up: counterclockwise -> normal points right (outward) ✓
    hex (5 6 10 9 13 14 15 11) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 12 8)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 11 15)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 13 12)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 9 8)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 7 6 5)
            (8 12 15 11)
            (5 9 10 13)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //"), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Wrong token type (expected Scalar, found comma)

**Error Message:**
```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 15 the punctuation token ','
```

**Location:** Line 15 of `blockMeshDict` in the `vertices` section.

### Diagnosis

Line 15 contains a vertex entry like:
```
(-1.0, -0.4, -0.07)   // 0
```

The error message says it expects a **Scalar** but found a **punctuation token ','. This is very strange because the format `(-1.0, -0.4, -0.07)` is the correct OpenFOAM vertex format. 

Upon closer inspection of the provided `blockMeshDict`, I notice the file has been heavily edited/mangled across attempts. The current file shows multiple `vertices` sections and the formatting looks corrupted. The actual error is likely due to **non-ASCII characters or malformed whitespace** in the vertex entries. Looking at the current file content, there appear to be stray characters, incorrect indentation, or the file has been concatenated with other content.

Specifically, looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the vertices section starts at line 15 with:
```
(-1.0, -0.4, -0.07)   // 0
(-0.6, -0.4, -0.07)   // 1
...
```

If the file has been corrupted (e.g., merged with other files, extra spaces, non-printable characters), the parser may see something that isn't a clean floating-point number. The error about "punctuation token ','" suggests the stream extraction for a `double` is failing because it encounters a comma where it expects a space or nothing.

### Solution

1. **Clean up the `blockMeshDict` file**: Remove any corruption, ensure proper ASCII formatting, and verify that each vertex entry is a clean triple like `(-1.0, -0.4, -0.07)` with proper spacing.

2. **Recommended approach**: Completely rewrite the `blockMeshDict` vertices and blocks section using a clean, minimal format. Based on the history of attempts, the root cause across all failures is that the vertex layout produces invalid blocks. Adopt the proven vertex/block layout from the previous successful attempt analysis:

```
vertices
(
    (-1.0, -0.4, -0.07)   // 0
    (-0.6, -0.4, -0.07)   // 1
    (0.6, -0.4, -0.07)    // 2
    (1.0, -0.4, -0.07)    // 3
    (-1.0, 0.4, -0.07)    // 4
    (-0.6, 0.4, -0.07)    // 5
    (0.6, 0.4, -0.07)     // 6
    (1.0, 0.4, -0.07)     // 7
    (-1.0, -0.4, 0.07)    // 8
    (-0.6, -0.4, 0.07)    // 9
    (0.6, -0.4, 0.07)     // 10
    (1.0, -0.4, 0.07)     // 11
    (-1.0, 0.4, 0.07)     // 12
    (-0.6, 0.4, 0.07)     // 13
    (0.6, 0.4, 0.07)      // 14
    (1.0, 0.4, 0.07)      // 15
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 5 4 8 9 13 12) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (1 2 6 5 9 10 14 13) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (4 5 9 8 12 13 14 15) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (5 6 10 9 13 14 15 11) (60 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

Ensure there are **no comments inline** (`//`) in the vertices section, as some versions of OpenFOAM may not parse them correctly. Place comments on separate lines if needed, or remove them entirely.

3. Ensure the file uses **straight ASCII commas** (`,`), not any Unicode or special comma variants.

---

## Error 2: `log.pimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

This is a **direct consequence** of `blockMesh` failing. No mesh was generated, so `pimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files. Once `blockMesh` succeeds, this resolves automatically.

---

## Summary of Required Fixes

1. **`blockMeshDict`**: Clean the file completely. Remove any corrupted/non-ASCII content. Rewrite the vertices and blocks sections using the clean layout shown above. Ensure no inline comments in the vertices block.

2. **All boundary condition files (`0/U`, `0/p`)**: Verify that the patch names in these files match exactly the boundary names defined in `blockMeshDict`: `inlet`, `outlet`, `top`, `bottom`, `frontAndBack`. Remove any references to patches like `cylinder`, `left`, `right`, or `defaultFaces` that don't exist in the mesh.</reviewer_analysis>

Use only 4 hex blocks: first block hex (0 1 5 4 8 9 13 12) with grading (60 20 1), second block hex (1 2 6 5 9 10 14 13) with grading (60 20 1), third block hex (4 5 9 8 12 13 14 15) with grading (60 20 1), fourth block hex (5 6 10 9 13 14 15 11) with grading (60 20 1). Ensure no comments in the vertices section, use straight ASCII commas, and ensure boundary faces reference only valid vertex indices. Keep scale 1. <user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-03 in physical properties.</user_requirement>
